blocks.content: December came and with it the particular mercy of the end-of-semester grind — tests, projects, the cafeteria decorated with paper snowflakes that Eli claimed were a violation of the separation of church and state, which Savannah explained was not how that worked.
Things weren't okay between them, but they were getting there. Daisy said hey in homeroom and meant it.
Brooke asked him once, quietly, if he was alright.

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: You don't have to explain it. I think I get it.
She said it without bitterness, which was more than he deserved, and she moved on.
On the last day before winter break, Eli pulled Kit aside at the bike rack.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: For what it's worth. She still lights up when you walk in the room. She just doesn't let herself anymore.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I'm just saying what I see.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Why are you telling me this.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Because you're still the least self-aware person I know and someone's got to.
